scheme_name: Award of Pre-Matric Scholarship to Other Economically Backward Class (OEBC) Students
page_title: Award of Pre-Matric Scholarship to Other Economically Backward Class (OEBC) Students

details: The "Award of Pre-Matric Scholarship to Other Economically Backward Class (OEBC) Students" initiated by the Directorate of School Education, Union Territory of Puducherry, is designed to extend financial assistance to OEBC students, mitigating the risk of dropouts due to financial constraints. The scheme provides scholarships to students in standards 6 to 10, based on specific eligibility criteria.
benefits: For standards 6 to 8: ₹50 per month per student (₹500 per year per student).
For standards 9 & 10: ₹75 per month per student (₹750 per year per student).
* For ten months in an academic year.
The amount is disbursed directly into the Aadhaar-seeded bank account of the applicant through Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) and Public Financial Management System (PFMS).
eligibility: The applicant should be a citizen of India.
The applicant should be a native of the Union Territory of Puducherry by birth or by continuous residence for not less than five years.
The applicant should be studying from standard 6 to 10 and should have secured promotion to the next higher class.
The applicant should belong to the Other Economically Backward Class (OEBC).
The family income of the applicant should not exceed ₹75,000 per annum.

online: The interested applicant should take print of the

In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, paste the passport-sized photograph (signed across, if required), and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).

The interested applicant should visit the Head of the Institution of his/her respective school and submit the duly filled and signed application form and the documents. The Heads of the Institutions then send the applications to the Directorate of School Education through Google Forms.

documents_required: Nativity/Citizenship/Residential Certificate, issued by the Officer of the Revenue Department not below the rank of a Deputy Tahsildar.

faqs.question: What are the scholarship rates for students in standards 6 to 8 under this scheme?
faqs.answer: For standards 6 to 8, the scholarship amount is ₹50 per month per student, summing up to ₹500 per year per student.

faqs.question: How is the scholarship amount disbursed to the eligible students?
faqs.answer: The scholarship amount is disbursed directly into Aadhaar-seeded bank accounts through the Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) and Public Financial Management System (PFMS).

faqs.question: What is the duration of the scholarship benefits provided by the scheme?
faqs.answer: The benefits of the scholarship are valid for ten months in an academic year.

faqs.question: What is the specific process for registering a new membership on the e-District Portal?
faqs.answer: To register, visit the portal, click "Login," then "Register a New Membership." Fill in mandatory fields like Username, Password, Personal Details, and click "Signup" after agreeing to the declaration.

faqs.question: How can applicants track the status of their scholarship application?
faqs.answer: Applicants can track their application by visiting the Track Your Application page on the e-District Portal, using the application number received on their Registered Mobile Number/Email ID.

faqs.question: Is there a User Manual available for the online application process?
faqs.answer: Yes, applicants can access the User Manual for Online Application on the e-District Portal for guidance during the application process.

faqs.question: Are self-attested documents required during the application process?
faqs.answer: Yes, applicants may need to self-attest mandatory documents as specified in the application form.

faqs.question: Can you elaborate on the role of the Department of Information & Publicity in implementing this scholarship scheme?
faqs.answer: The Department of Information & Publicity, Union Territory of Puducherry, is the implementing authority, overseeing the design and execution of the scholarship scheme to benefit OEBC students.

faqs.question: What are the specific criteria for the applicant's residence in the Union Territory of Puducherry?
faqs.answer: The applicant should either be a native of Puducherry by birth or have continuous residence for not less than five years in the Union Territory.

faqs.question: Is there any specific age limit mentioned for applicants under this scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme does not explicitly mention an age limit, focusing on students in standards 6 to 10.

faqs.question: Is there a helpline or customer support service available for applicants with queries?
faqs.answer: The Citizen's Charter & Contact Details on the official website provide information about helpline numbers and customer support services for any queries applicants may have.

faqs.question: Is there any specific provision for students with disabilities under this scholarship scheme?
faqs.answer: While the scheme does not explicitly mention provisions for students with disabilities, applicants are encouraged to review the guidelines or contact the authorities for specific queries.
